Friday Fifteen Links: July 6th Edition

July 7, 2012

Each Friday I am going to attempt to give you high quality reading material for the weekend, in the form of fifteen links from the previous week.

Here we go — I hope you enjoy:

Technology/Business/Productivity/Etc.

  • How we avoid ‘Job Title Creep’ at our startup
  • Why Killing Time Isn’t a Sin
  • What multitasking does to our brains
  • The Wisdom of Allowing Things to Happen
  • The long-overdue reaction against being completely overwhelmed

Politics

  • Mitt Romney and the New Gilded Age
  • No, ‘Obamacare’ isn’t ‘the largest tax increase in the history of the world’ (in one chart)
  • Mitt Romney’s bogus attack on the Affordable Care Act
  • Dear Romney Campaign, This Is How You Make a Venn Diagram
  • Off and Out With Mitt Romney
  • States Bucking ‘Obamacare’ Are Among Those With Highest Rates Of Uninsured
  • The Nominee Who Had A Swiss Bank Account
  • Does Medicaid Make People Healthier?
  • Texas Republicans call for an end to critical thinking in schools
  • The truth about Medicaid’s cost to states, in three charts
